title: 2xCTAB Protocol for (simultaneous) DNA Isolation from Aiptasia and Symbiodinium
author: Pringle Lab
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.qx6dxre
materials: Screw-cap or safe-lock microcentrifuge tubes (1.5-2 ml)

steps.title: Homogenize Sample
steps.instruction: Homogenize anemone with a rotor-stator in 500 µl 2× CTAB buffer individually in screw-cap or safe-lock 1.5-2 ml microcentrifuge tubes. This is important for the Phenol extraction; simple tubes might leak!Make sure your tubes tolerate the chemicals and centrifugation forces! Make sure to clean the rotor thouroughly afterwards. The 2-Mercaptoethanol in the CTAB buffer is toxic if inhaled, swallowed or at skin contact. https://chemicalsafety.com/sds1/sdsviewer.php?id=30038887&name=2-Mercaptoethanol

steps.title: Shear DNA
steps.instruction: Do 5 rounds of sheering through a 25-gauge needle. Important: DNA in host-algae homogenate in SDS or water will degrade rapidly with every freeze-thaw cycle! If you want to perform DNA isolation from these samples later, freeze the samples after step 6.

steps.title: Add Glass Beads
steps.instruction: Add ~200 µl of glass beads (0.5 mm).

steps.title: Vortex Sample
steps.instruction: Vortex for 1-5 min at ≥2000 rpm until no cell clumps are visible.

steps.title: Add Proteinase K
steps.instruction: Add 3.6 µl of Proteinase K (20 mg/ml). Mix by inverting several times.

steps.title: Incubate with Proteinase K
steps.instruction: Incubate at 65°C for 30-60 min. Invert occasionally while incubating. The suspension should become green and less opaque when the cells lyse. 20 min should be enough for less dense samples. 00:30:00, 65°C incubation

steps.title: Perform Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol Extraction
steps.instruction: Add 600 µl Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1, TE-buffered to pH ≥ 7, very TOXIC!!! ), mix thoroughly by vortexing several seconds. This should result in a milky emulsion with two phases starting to form. Phenol is carcinogenic and causes chemical burns at skin contact. Chloroform is a carcinogen and an irritant. https://chemicalsafety.com/sds1/sdsviewer.php?id=30148468&name=Phenol%20–%20chloroform%20–%20isoamyl%20alcohol%20mixture Phenol is carcinogenic and causes chemical burns at skin contact. Chloroform is a carcinogen and an irritant. https://chemicalsafety.com/sds1/sdsviewer.php?id=30148468&name=Phenol%20–%20chloroform%20–%20isoamyl%20alcohol%20mixture

steps.title: Centrifuge for Phase Separation
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 10 min at 14,000 ×g to separate the phases.

steps.title: Transfer Aqueous Phase
steps.instruction: Take ~550 µl of the aqueous, upper phase without disturbing the interphase and transfer to a new tube.

steps.title: Add Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol
steps.instruction: Add 500 µl of Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (24:1), mix thoroughly by vortexing several seconds. You can also use plain Chloroform. Chloroform is a carcinogen and an irritant. https://chemicalsafety.com/sds1/sdsviewer.php?id=30148458&name=Chloroform%20–%20isoamyl%20alcohol%20mixture

steps.title: Centrifuge Sample
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 10 min at 14,000 ×g.

steps.title: Transfer Aqueous Phase Again
steps.instruction: Take ~450 µl of the aqueous, upper phase and transfer to a new tube. Do NOT disturb the interphase!

steps.title: Add 100% Ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml of 100% ethanol (molecular biology grade) and mix well.

steps.title: Ethanol Incubation
steps.instruction: Incubate for ≥30 min at RT. 00:30:00, Incubation at room temperature

steps.title: Centrifuge for DNA Pellet
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for ≥30 min at 14,000 ×g at RT. Be sure to orient all tubes in the same direction to know on which side the pellet will form.

steps.title: Decant Supernatant
steps.instruction: Decant supernatant. You might not see any pellet at this step. Just be careful to not scratch off the DNA from the side where the pellet should be.

steps.title: Wash with 70% Ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 500 µl 70% ethanol, don’t mix, centrifuge for 5 min at 14,000 ×g.

steps.title: Remove Supernatant Carefully
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant carefully with a pipet, without disturbing the pellet. It helps to use a 1000 µl pipette with a respective tip and add a 200 µl tip.

steps.title: Air-Dry DNA Pellet
steps.instruction: Air-dry for 10 min or until no Ethanol is visible. 00:10:00, Drying

steps.title: Resuspend DNA
steps.instruction: Add 30 µl ddH 2 O or 10 mM Tris/HCl pH 8.5 to the pellet. You can also use TE (Tris-EDTA) buffer but be aware that the EDTA might interfere with downstream enzymatic reactions.
